raw_consumer_statement: in XX/XX/year>, I initiated a dispute for merchandise paid for not received. This is in regards to an XXXX XXXX order that was purchased for in store but shipped to me since they did not have the specific merchandise in store. These items were purchased while traveling and were not received by me until I returned home, as it was an online order placed through the store. When returning home, I had found out the wrong items were shipped to me. The company stated that they can not issue me a refund since I am past the timeframe allowed for returns. I then initiated a dispute through my bank. They then found the dispute to be in favor of the merchant since it was an in-store purchase and to be claimed that I could have reviewed the merchandise before purchasing. I immediately re-initiated this dispute and mentioned that there was no merchandise received in store, it was an online order although the payment happened in store. The bank then soon after less than a week closed the dispute and stated a list of reasons of why the dispute was not founded my favor, although I was not given the opportunity to submit any documents. This bank is extremely difficult to work with hence why I closed my account with them. They did not give me the option to upload documents by being signed in or upload any documents by a link being sent to my email. Although I did thoroughly state all of my documentation verbally to them when creating my dispute case. I even called the bank and asked why this was not found in my favor, and the woman stated that the bank has to abide by the merchants policies, and if I missed the timeframe for returns, then this is found in the merchant favor. I then countered that and stated that I never received what I purchased as they sent me the wrong items. I proceeded to state that the merchant also had an agreement to ship me the items I purchased, although they failed to do that, and additionally stated that in all XXXX states it is common law that no business is entitled to keep money charged by a customer for products or services that are not received, then asked again how it was found in the merchants favor. She was in silent for about XXXX seconds until the call ended.
